task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the required layers with their specified colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List panel in LibreCAD. You should see layers named 'WALLS' (Black/White), 'DUCT-SUPPLY' (Cyan), 'DUCT-DIFFUSER' (Red), 'DUCT-RETURN' (Magenta), and 'DUCT-LABELS' (Green).

task_completion.description: Verify the room outline is drawn on the WALLS layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a large outer rectangle (360x240 units) drawn in black/white representing the room boundaries.

task_completion.description: Verify the main horizontal supply trunk is drawn on the DUCT-SUPPLY layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a long horizontal cyan rectangle (representing a 24-inch high duct) running across the middle-lower section of the room.

task_completion.description: Verify the four vertical supply branches are drawn on the DUCT-SUPPLY layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for four vertical cyan rectangles (representing 10-inch wide ducts) extending upwards and downwards from the main horizontal trunk.

task_completion.description: Verify the four diffusers are drawn on the DUCT-DIFFUSER layer at the ends of the branches.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for four red squares, each containing an 'X' inside, located at the outer ends of the four vertical cyan branches.

task_completion.description: Verify the return grille box is drawn on the DUCT-RETURN layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a magenta square containing three horizontal lines inside it, located near the right side of the main trunk.

task_completion.description: Verify the duct size text labels are added on the DUCT-LABELS layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for green text reading '24x12' near the main horizontal trunk and '10x8' near one of the vertical branches.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the LibreCAD graphical user interface to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the LibreCAD application window with its toolbars, drawing area, and layer panels, rather than a terminal window running a script or a text editor modifying a DXF file directly.

integrity.description: Verify the drawing consists of actual CAD geometric entities.
integrity.visual_evidence: The shapes (rectangles, lines, text) should appear in the main drawing canvas of LibreCAD as vector graphics, not as pasted images or plain text descriptions.

integrity.description: Verify the file is saved with the correct name.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the LibreCAD window or a save dialog box to confirm the file is named 'hvac_duct_layout.dxf'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/hvac_duct_layout.dxf. The DXF file must contain five specific layers: WALLS (Black/White), DUCT-SUPPLY (Cyan), DUCT-DIFFUSER (Red), DUCT-RETURN (Magenta), and DUCT-LABELS (Green). The complete drawing should contain approximately 57 geometric entities (minimum 35 for partial credit), including a 360x240 room rectangle, a main supply trunk, 4 branch ducts, 4 diffusers (squares with X marks), 1 return grille (square with 3 horizontal lines), and 2 text labels ('24x12' and '10x8').
